qa_pairs.question: What are the initial requirements for installing Materials Studio on Alliance clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: The Alliance does not install Materials Studio centrally, so users must possess a valid licence and install the application in their own account. Before beginning, if you are using the default 2020 standard software environment, you will need to load an older standard software environment by executing `module load StdEnv/2016.4`.

qa_pairs.question: What are the specific steps to install Materials Studio 2020 for an individual user on an Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: To install Materials Studio 2020, you will need the installer archive, `BIOVIA_2020.MaterialsStudio2020.tar`, and the IP address or DNS name, along with the port of your configured licence server. First, upload the `.tar` file to your `/home` folder on the intended cluster. Next, run the commands `export MS_LICENCE_SERVER=<port>@<server>` and `eb MaterialsStudio-2020.eb --sourcepath=$HOME`. After this process, log out of the cluster and log back in. You can then load the module using `module load materialsstudio/2020`. Lastly, to ensure access to the licence server from compute nodes, you must contact technical support for firewall configuration.

qa_pairs.question: How can a Principal Investigator (PI) install Materials Studio for their entire research group to use on an Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: As a PI holding the Materials Studio licence, you can perform a single installation for your group in a `/project` directory. First, determine the actual path of your project directory, setting it as `PI_PROJECT_DIR`, and identify its group, setting it as `PI_GROUP`. Then, change your default group to your team's `def-` group (e.g., `newgrp $PI_GROUP`), open permissions for your project directory (e.g., `chmod g+rsx $PI_PROJECT_DIR`), and create an installation directory within `/project` (e.g., `mkdir $PI_PROJECT_DIR/MatStudio2018`). Finally, install the software using `MS_LICENCE_SERVER=<port>@<server> eb MaterialsStudio-2018-dummy-dummy.eb --installpath=$PI_PROJECT_DIR/MatStudio2018 --sourcepath=$HOME`. For team members to use the software, they need to run `module use $PI_PROJECT_DIR/MatStudio2018/modules/2017/Core/` (which can be added to their `~/.bashrc` file) and then `module load materialsstudio`.
